channel.title: AI+Database Research Podcast
channel.description: A podcast series exploring the intersection of AI and Database systems — surveys, benchmarks, frontier paradigms, and open research questions. Generated from academic papers using NotebookLM, curated for PhD students and researchers entering the field.
channel.itunes:author: Xinyang Zhang
channel.itunes:summary: A podcast series exploring the intersection of AI and Database systems — surveys, benchmarks, frontier paradigms, and open research questions. Curated for PhD students and researchers entering the field.

item.title: Autonomous Databases and the LLM Frontier
item.description: An introductory overview of the AI+Database research field for a first-year PhD student. Covers: AI4DB vs DB4AI, major research lines including text-to-SQL, learned database components, AI-native data systems, data agents, and LLM+data integration. Based on three foundational survey papers.
item.content:encoded:
An introductory overview of the AI+Database research field for a first-year PhD student.

Covers: AI4DB vs DB4AI, major research lines including text-to-SQL, learned database components, AI-native data systems, data agents, and LLM+data integration.

Based on three foundational survey papers: "AI Meets Database" (Li et al., SIGMOD 2021), "Database Meets AI" (Zhou et al., 2021), and "A Survey of LLM x DATA" (Zhou et al., 2025).
item.itunes:summary: An introductory overview of the AI+Database research field. Covers AI4DB vs DB4AI, major research lines, and open questions from three foundational survey papers.
item.itunes:author: Xinyang Zhang

item.title: Why LLMs Fail at Enterprise SQL
item.description: A deep dive into Text-to-SQL — the most studied problem at the AI+Database intersection. Covers: the evolution from rule-based to LLM-powered approaches, the benchmark ecosystem (BIRD, Spider, Spider 2.0), key technical challenges, and why enterprise-grade Text-to-SQL remains far from solved. Based on five key papers.
item.content:encoded:
A deep dive into Text-to-SQL — the most studied problem at the AI+Database intersection.

Covers: the evolution from rule-based to LLM-powered approaches, the benchmark ecosystem (BIRD, Spider, Spider 2.0), key technical challenges (schema linking, complex SQL, dirty databases), what DAIL-SQL revealed about prompt engineering, and the sobering reality that enterprise-grade Text-to-SQL remains far from solved.

Based on five papers: "Next-Generation Database Interfaces" (Hong et al., TKDE 2025), "BIRD" (Li et al., NeurIPS 2024), "DAIL-SQL" (Gao et al., VLDB 2024), "NL2SQL: State of the Art" (Luo et al., VLDB 2025), and "Spider 2.0" (Lei et al., 2025).
item.itunes:summary: A deep dive into Text-to-SQL: benchmarks, LLM approaches, key challenges, and why enterprise NL2SQL is still far from solved. Based on five key papers.
item.itunes:author: Xinyang Zhang
